title: Cardiopulmonary Rehabilitation, Tilt-Table Testing & Hemodynamic Monitoring Quiz

questions.text: Cardiac rehabilitation exercise training was introduced after which decade?
Patients who exercise as part of cardiac rehabilitation experience approximately what reduction in mortality and recurrent MIs?
VO2 max is the maximal amount of oxygen an individual can transport during exercise before being limited by:
VO2 is directly related to which two hemodynamic values?
During cardiac rehabilitation, increasing VO2 submaximal results in:

answers.text: Decreased dyspnea and fatigue during everyday activities

questions.text: Phase I of cardiac rehabilitation is best described as:

answers.text: Physician-supervised outpatient exercise with ECG monitoring
Non-ECG monitored maintenance at a health club or gym
Inpatient program started soon after the acute cardiac event, usually by physical therapy

questions.text: Phase II of cardiac rehabilitation typically involves exercising how often, for how long?

answers.text: Three times weekly for 3–4 months with ECG monitoring

questions.text: Phase III of cardiac rehabilitation is characterized by:

answers.text: Non-ECG monitored maintenance, usually at health clubs or gyms
Daily ECG monitoring at a cardiology clinic

questions.text: The primary purpose of tilt-table testing is to identify patients who have a vasodepressor or cardio-inhibitory response as a cause of:
During a tilt-table test, the table is tilted upright to a maximum of how many degrees, for how long?
If a patient has no reaction during tilt-table testing, which drug may be administered to provoke syncope?
The three common indwelling catheters used for hemodynamic monitoring are:

answers.text: A-line, CVP catheter, and PAP (Swan-Ganz) catheter
A-line, endotracheal tube, and CVP catheter
PAP catheter, Foley catheter, and A-line

questions.text: Arterial lines are most commonly placed in which arteries?
CVP catheter access typically begins in which vessels?
The tip of a CVP catheter is located in the:
A PICC line differs from a tunneled catheter in that it is placed:

answers.text: Entirely under the skin in the chest
In a vein in the arm

questions.text: On a Swan-Ganz catheter, the BLUE port is used to measure:

answers.text: CVP / RAP (Central Venous Pressure / Right Atrial Pressure)

questions.text: On a Swan-Ganz catheter, the RED port is used to measure:
PCWP is clinically valuable primarily for differentiating between:
The device that converts catheter-measured pressures into interpretable numbers on the monitor is the:
The normal range for mean arterial pressure (MAP) is:
Hypertension is defined as an arterial BP greater than:
Hypotension (MAP < 80 mmHg) can be caused by all of the following EXCEPT:
The formula for Cardiac Output (CO) is:
In the thermodilution method for measuring CO, what solution is injected through the Swan-Ganz catheter?
Cardiac Index (CI) is calculated as:
The correction factor of 80 in the SVR and PVR formulas is used to convert mmHg into:
The formula for Systemic Vascular Resistance (SVR) is:
The formula for Pulmonary Vascular Resistance (PVR) is:
